challenge_summary: The plan gets the broad security shape right, but it is still too loose where Azure Policy and identity flows require exact implementation detail. The main blockers are an ambiguous 9-tag contract, an AAD-only SQL design that never provisions the App Service managed identity inside the database, and a Key Vault design that disables public access without defining a working private or VNet access path.
compact_for_parent: Pass 1 (security-governance) | HIGH | 3 must_fix, 3 should_fix | Key: Tag contract ambiguous; SQL MI user missing; Key Vault access path broken

issues.title: Tag contract leaves deny-policy tag names ambiguous
issues.description: The plan repeatedly says modules receive 'all 11 tags' but never enumerates the 9 deny-enforced resource-group tag keys. The governance artifacts already contain an inconsistency between technical-contact and tech-contact.
issues.artifact_section: Module Structure > Module Interface Contract; Task 1; Task 10
issues.suggested_mitigation: Define the 9 policy-required tag keys verbatim in the plan: environment, owner, costcenter, application, workload, sla, backup-policy, maint-window, technical-contact.

issues.title: AAD-only SQL plan omits contained user creation for App Service MI
issues.description: The SQL task correctly sets azureADOnlyAuthentication: true but the plan never defines how the App Service managed identity becomes a contained database user with least-privilege roles.
issues.suggested_mitigation: Add explicit post-deployment step to create the App Service MI as a contained database user and grant only required roles (db_datareader, db_datawriter).

issues.title: Key Vault is locked down without a working private access path
issues.description: The Key Vault task sets publicNetworkAccess: Disabled and networkAcls.defaultAction: Deny in production, but creates no Key Vault PE, no privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net DNS zone, and no VNet/service-endpoint alternative.
issues.artifact_section: Task 4: keyvault.bicep; Task 8: compute.bicep; Security Configuration
issues.suggested_mitigation: Add a Key Vault PE + privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net private DNS and VNet link, or explicitly configure service endpoints and virtualNetworkRules.

issues.title: Network isolation matrix overstates subnet hardening
issues.description: snet-app has no NSG even though the governance summary says NSGs exist on all subnets. Dev collapses to a single subnet without documented compliance exception.
issues.artifact_section: Task 2: network.bicep; Security Configuration > Network Isolation
issues.suggested_mitigation: Attach an NSG to the app subnet and document whether dev is a non-PCI/non-production-data environment.

issues.title: Diagnostics are not actually planned for all resources
issues.description: Task-level plan only defines diagnostics for SQL, Storage, and Compute. Key Vault, VNet/NSGs, Private DNS zones, Private Endpoints, and Activity Log routing are absent.
issues.artifact_section: Tasks 2, 4, 5; Runtime Flow Diagram; Security Configuration
issues.suggested_mitigation: Add per-resource diagnostic settings for Key Vault AuditEvent, App Service logs, NSG flow logs, and Activity Log routing to Log Analytics.

issues.title: Budget monitoring misses mandatory anomaly and escalation controls
issues.description: Budget task hardcodes amount and contact email, adds no action group escalation for 100%/120% thresholds, and does not include anomaly detection.
issues.suggested_mitigation: Parameterize budgetAmount/contacts, add action group for 100%/120% thresholds, include anomaly detection notification.
